# Scorched-Earth Erdős Sweep — Turn 6

This is turn 6. Turns 1 through 5 are consolidated in `turns_history__q2_refinement.md` (attached). Re-read it before answering.
Also re-read `mega_prompt.md` and `problem_statement.md`.

CRITICAL — use ONLY the files attached to THIS message. Do NOT open, import, retrieve, or cite any file from your File Library / previously uploaded files, even one named similarly (e.g. another `turns_history…md`). Other unrelated problems use similarly named history files in the same account, and importing one will silently switch you onto the wrong problem. If the attached `turns_history__q2_refinement.md` or `current_manuscript.tex` appears missing, do NOT continue from partial context. Reply only with `MISSING_REQUIRED_ATTACHMENT` and name the missing file(s); never substitute a library file.

If this turn drifts into hammering one specific case or sub-instance, step back and ask whether the case is revealing a structural pattern that would close many cases at once. Prefer arguments whose conclusion is a uniform statement.

End this response with ESTABLISHED / GAP / NEXT + tag.
Continue with Turn 6.

## Turn 6 — citation and dependency audit complete

The attached manuscript was still the conditional version containing the false Proposition 3.4 and the obsolete insertion-point language. I replaced that subsection with the direct marked-isomorphism argument developed in Turns 3–5.  

The revision now:

- proves \(\operatorname{Aut}(D_0)\twoheadrightarrow\operatorname{Aut}(D_0^{\mathrm{ab}},\chi_0)\) uniformly;
- uses the corrected shear
  \[
  A\mapsto A^{S^b},\qquad S\mapsto S,\qquad Y\mapsto YS^b,
  \]
  which sends \(A^2S^4[S,Y]\) to its \(S^b\)-conjugate;
- adds the local orientation row and the pinpoint Labute/Deligne references;
- deletes Proposition 3.4 and every remaining conditional or “insertion point” reference;
- leaves the displayed candidate and machine-readable relation unchanged.

The source compiles in three clean passes to a 40-page PDF, with no undefined references, citation warnings, overfull boxes, or obsolete-label occurrences. I also rendered the replacement section and final pages to check the layout.

[Patched LaTeX](sandbox:/mnt/data/current_manuscript_turn6_patched.tex) · [Compiled PDF](sandbox:/mnt/data/current_manuscript_turn6_patched.pdf) · [Unified patch](sandbox:/mnt/data/current_manuscript_turn6.patch)

ESTABLISHED — The marked dyadic Demushkin normalization is now proved directly and integrated; the candidate presentation is unchanged.  
GAP — No internal algebraic or dependency gap was found in the replacement argument during this sweep.  
NEXT — Freeze the normalization section and subject the remaining global finite-quotient induction to an independent end-to-end referee audit.  
[INTEGRATED]
